cross_cutting_themes: Packet lineage and evidence ledgers drifted from actual implementation state in both 013 and 014.
Public contract changes did not propagate to downstream READMEs, tool schemas, feature catalog pages, and manual playbooks.
Regression and manual verification coverage lagged behind the changed behavior, leaving multiple fixes unguarded.
Cross-runtime parity remains incomplete where shared renderers, startup/runtime coverage, and OpenCode bridge documentation disagree.
Core handler correctness and contract-boundary issues remain in live code paths for both code-graph and skill-advisor.

findings.summary: The public `advisor_recommend` cache key ignores explicit `workspaceRoot`, so one process can reuse recommendations across repositories when freshness state matches.
findings.recommended_fix: Include resolved `workspaceRoot` in the recommendation cache key or use the workspace-scoped freshness source signature, then add a multi-workspace cache isolation regression test.

findings.summary: Ambiguous CALLS/import subject resolution still ranks only the first 10 SQL matches, so the operation-aware selector can miss the correct implementation symbol in larger ambiguity sets.
findings.recommended_fix: Re-rank the full ambiguous match set instead of truncating to the first 10 SQL rows, and add a >10-candidate regression for the new operation-aware selector.

findings.summary: The packet preserves CocoIndex seed fidelity only when callers send `seed.file`; `seed.filePath` falls through the generic seed path and drops provider-specific metadata.
findings.recommended_fix: Normalize `filePath` and `file` equivalently for CocoIndex seeds and add direct regression coverage for the `filePath` form.

findings.summary: The new `partialOutput` contract undercounts omitted anchors when the deadline expires before multiple remaining anchors are processed.
findings.recommended_fix: Count all remaining anchors when deadline pressure interrupts the build loop and add a multi-anchor timeout regression.

findings.summary: No-op incremental scans can report `filesSkipped: 0` and clear the persisted edge-enrichment summary even though fresh files were skipped before parsing and no new graph state was written.
findings.recommended_fix: Track pre-parse skipped files separately from persisted results and only clear the stored enrichment summary when a replacement graph state is actually persisted.

findings.summary: The packet claims packet-local `applied/T-*.md` evidence throughout the closeout docs, but those files are not present under the packet path, so the implementation cannot be fully audited from this checkout.
findings.recommended_fix: Restore the missing packet-local applied reports or replace every evidence reference with proof that is actually present inside the packet.

findings.summary: The central MCP server README still documents `code_graph_status` and `code_graph_context` at the pre-013 contract level, so operators do not see `graphQualitySummary`, blocked-read payloads, or structured `partialOutput` in the main reference.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the README sections for `code_graph_status` and `code_graph_context` so they name `graphQualitySummary`, blocked-read payloads, and `partialOutput` metadata.

findings.summary: The code-graph subsystem README still presents the older status/readiness surface and omits the post-013 status, blocked-read, and startup-quality details.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the subsystem README to cover `graphQualitySummary`, blocked full-scan payloads, and structured context metadata alongside the existing readiness overview.

findings.summary: The install guide still explains only freshness/full-scan behavior, so operators do not get the newer 013-era status/context vocabulary in the troubleshooting path.
findings.recommended_fix: Extend the install guide's code-graph sections so they mention `graphQualitySummary` and the blocked-read/context metadata operators will actually encounter.

findings.summary: The public tool-schema descriptions still describe the old `code_graph_status` and `code_graph_context` outputs, so generated tool docs and downstream consumers inherit stale contract text.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the tool descriptions so `code_graph_status` advertises `graphQualitySummary` and `code_graph_context` advertises blocked-read plus `partialOutput` behavior.

findings.summary: The feature-catalog page for code-graph storage/query still points at the old indexer test path and describes `code_graph_status` generically, so the curated feature reference is stale.
findings.recommended_fix: Correct the test path and refresh the feature description so it matches the live `code_graph_status` and `code_graph_context` contract.

findings.summary: The CocoIndex bridge feature page still points at the old seed-resolver test path and does not capture the richer post-013 context contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Correct the test path and extend the bridge description so it reflects seed-fidelity plus blocked/partial-output behavior in `code_graph_context`.

findings.summary: The startup manual-testing playbook still validates only the old tool-listing and budget contract, even though packet 013 added startup payload and graph-quality assertions in the hook test suite.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the startup playbook so manual validation covers startup payload/graph-quality assertions in addition to tool listing and token budget.

findings.summary: The storage/query playbook still uses the old `tests/code-graph-indexer.vitest.ts` path and validates counts-only status behavior, so the operator scenario lags the live packet 013 contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Move the playbook to the real test path and add `graphQualitySummary` expectations for `code_graph_status`.

findings.summary: The semantic-vs-structural routing playbook still uses the old indexer test path and never mentions the 013-era `partialOutput` or blocked-read contract for `code_graph_context`.
findings.recommended_fix: Point the playbook at the real suites and extend it so routing validation covers `partialOutput` plus blocked-read expectations, not only non-empty mode output.

findings.summary: Packet 014's governing spec still defines the work as research-only and out-of-scope for implementation, but the packet now claims completed implementation and verification inside the same folder.
findings.recommended_fix: Either update packet 014's governing spec and metadata to describe the shipped implementation, or move this implementation summary/checklist under a follow-up child packet whose spec explicitly authorizes the code changes.

findings.summary: The packet marks T-001 through T-015 complete and cites `applied/T-###.md` evidence, but no `applied/` directory exists, so the verification ledger is missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Restore the missing `applied/T-###.md` evidence set or correct the checklist, implementation summary, and resource map so they only claim evidence that actually exists on disk.

findings.summary: Packet 014 did not fully converge on a shared renderer: the builder still emits a private brief format and stores it in shared payload, while hooks render a different prompt-visible brief through `renderAdvisorBrief(...)`.
findings.recommended_fix: Route the builder through `renderAdvisorBrief(...)` and use the same rendered string for shared payload summary and section content so resume consumers and hook users observe one contract.

findings.summary: The packet's Codex verification narrative only proves fail-open `{}` behavior and missing packet-local evidence, not the successful shared-brief path the implementation claims to normalize.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace the packet-local Codex smoke claims with explicit success-path evidence or a restored `applied/T-013.md` ledger that shows which hook and prompt-wrapper scenarios actually ran for packet 014.

findings.summary: Durable outcome telemetry is not corruption-tolerant: one malformed JSONL line can make `advisor_validate` throw and take down the validation surface.
findings.recommended_fix: Parse and validate outcome JSONL defensively, drop malformed lines, and add a validator regression test that seeds corrupt telemetry data.

findings.summary: `skillSlug` validation scopes corpus rows only; telemetry outcome totals remain workspace-global, which makes subset validation results misleading.
findings.recommended_fix: Either scope outcome totals to the selected `skillSlug` or rename/document them as workspace-global telemetry and add a subset-validation regression test.

findings.summary: The OpenCode plugin-bridge feature catalog page still points at the old `.opencode/plugins/` helper location and the pre-014 `0.7` threshold wording.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the feature-catalog leaf to the `.opencode/plugin-helpers/` bridge path and align its threshold wording with the live helper and 014 regression contract.

findings.summary: The OpenCode bridge manual playbook still tells operators to invoke the old bridge path with `thresholdConfidence:0.7`.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace the bridge command and expected threshold wording in the manual playbook with the live helper path and 014 threshold pair.

findings.summary: The `advisor_recommend` feature catalog page still describes the pre-014 public output shape and omits `workspaceRoot` plus `effectiveThresholds`.
findings.recommended_fix: Expand the feature-catalog entry so it matches the landed `advisor_recommend` contract, especially `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds`.

findings.summary: The native `advisor_recommend` manual scenario still validates only the recommendation payload and does not check the new public 014 output fields.
findings.recommended_fix: Add explicit expected checks for `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds` to the recommend happy-path playbook.

findings.summary: The `advisor_validate` feature catalog page still reflects the older slice-only description and omits `thresholdSemantics`, telemetry diagnostics, and outcome totals.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the feature-catalog page so it documents the landed validate contract, including threshold semantics and prompt-safe telemetry/outcome rollups.

findings.summary: The native `advisor_validate` playbook still checks only slice presence and baseline metrics, not the 014 telemetry and outcome surfaces.
findings.recommended_fix: Expand the validate playbook to verify `thresholdSemantics`, telemetry diagnostics, and recorded outcome totals alongside the existing slice checks.

findings.summary: Startup parity is claimed across four runtimes, but the packet's direct test inventory only records startup hook regressions for Claude and Codex.
findings.recommended_fix: Add packet-local Gemini and Copilot startup hook regressions, or narrow the closeout language to the runtimes with direct assertions.

findings.summary: `implementation-summary.md` omits `hooks/codex/session-start.ts` from `Files Changed` even though the task list and resource map both treat it as part of the startup-parity implementation.
findings.recommended_fix: Add the missing Codex runtime adapter row to `implementation-summary.md` so the closeout inventory matches the actual blast radius.

findings.summary: The ambiguity regression suite does not cover match sets larger than the new hard candidate cap, so the ranking defect in `F-001` is not currently guarded.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a large-ambiguity regression that proves the selector still picks the intended implementation node beyond 10 matches.

findings.summary: The context handler's new partial-output coverage does not include a multi-anchor deadline case, leaving the `omittedAnchors` bug from `F-003` unguarded.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a multi-anchor timeout regression that asserts `partialOutput.omittedAnchors` matches the number of skipped anchors.

findings.summary: The scan regression suite mocks `indexFiles()` in a way that bypasses the real `skipFreshFiles` contract, so the no-op incremental bug from `F-004` remains untested.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a no-op incremental regression where `indexFiles()` returns an empty result set and assert summary preservation plus correct skipped-file accounting.

findings.summary: The handler-local README for `code-graph/handlers/` still summarizes `status.ts` and `context.ts` generically after packet 013 changed those surfaces materially.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the handler-local README to reflect the post-013 status/context/query surface.

findings.summary: The readiness-contract feature page still stops at shared helper fields and an old sibling-readiness test path, even though callers now see richer blocked-read payloads on top of that layer.
findings.recommended_fix: Extend the readiness-contract page so it documents the richer blocked-read payload surface and current supporting test locations.

findings.summary: The auto-trigger feature page still frames stale/empty handling purely as helper actions, not as the explicit blocked query/context payloads that operators now encounter after packet 013.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the auto-trigger feature page so it teaches the caller-visible blocked query/context payload contract, not only helper actions.

findings.summary: The auto-trigger manual-testing playbook still validates action-only helper outcomes and the old indexer-suite path instead of the newer blocked query/context contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the auto-trigger manual-testing playbook so it validates the blocked query/context contract and current supporting test paths.

findings.summary: The packet resource map is no longer a clean ledger: it mixes narrative and unresolved `NEEDS VERIFICATION` notes with stale research lineage, and it also reports non-existent artifacts as present.
findings.recommended_fix: Regenerate the resource map as a purely factual path ledger, reconcile the canonical research lineage, and remove inline audit judgments from the file.

findings.summary: The `advisor_recommend` happy-path handler test never asserts the new public `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds` fields.
findings.recommended_fix: Add contract assertions for `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds` to the happy-path test so future recommend-output drift is caught automatically.

findings.summary: The setup guide still uses stale OpenCode bridge wording after the helper relocation, even though the plugin README is already correct.
findings.recommended_fix: Align the OpenCode row in `SET-UP_GUIDE.md` with the helper layout already documented in `.opencode/plugins/README.md`.
